<!-- review_stack_entry_start -->

[![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/hbmartin/connectbot/pull/32?utm_source=github_walkthrough&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=change_stack)

<!-- review_stack_entry_end -->
<!-- walkthrough_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Walkthrough</summary>

## Walkthrough

This change adds mDNS (.local) hostname resolution used by SSH connections and jump hosts, introducing `MdnsResolver` and `MdnsProxyData`, plus a new `NETWORK_UNREACHABLE` disconnect reason with SSH-side classification. It adds a `CONNECTING` host-list connection state with amber UI styling, moves host "connected timestamp" recording to a successful-connection callback, fixes Enter-key handling to clear active console selection, and adds host-editor field persistence across process death via `SavedStateHandle`. Supporting manifest permission, string, and color resources are included, along with corresponding unit tests.

### Sequence Diagram(s)

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant SSH
  participant MdnsProxyData
  participant MdnsResolver
  participant Socket

  SSH->>SSH: mdnsProxyData(host)
  SSH->>MdnsProxyData: setProxyData(resolver, ipVersion)
  MdnsProxyData->>MdnsResolver: resolve(hostname, ipVersion)
  alt platform resolution succeeds
    MdnsResolver-->>MdnsProxyData: InetAddress
  else fallback to multicast query
    MdnsResolver->>MdnsResolver: queryViaMulticast (UDP 5353)
    MdnsResolver-->>MdnsProxyData: InetAddress or null
  end
  MdnsProxyData->>Socket: connect(resolvedAddress, port)
  Socket-->>SSH: connected or IOException
  SSH->>SSH: classify failure via isNetworkUnreachable -> DisconnectReason
```

**Possibly related PRs**
- [hbmartin/connectbot#24](https://github.com/hbmartin/connectbot/pull/24): Extends the same disconnect-reason and SSH connect-error classification flow (`DisconnectReason.kt`, SSH connect-time error handling) that this PR builds upon with `NETWORK_UNREACHABLE`.

</details>

<!-- walkthrough_end -->

<sub>Comment `@coderabbitai help` to get the list of available commands.</sub>

<!-- tips_end -->

## Code Review

This pull request introduces local mDNS resolution for `.local` hostnames using a custom `MdnsResolver` and `MdnsProxyData`, updates connection state handling to include a distinct `CONNECTING` state, and ensures host connection timestamps are only updated upon successful connection. It also adds state preservation in `HostEditorViewModel` to survive process death and improves connect-time failure reporting with a new `NETWORK_UNREACHABLE` status. Feedback on the changes suggests catching general exceptions in `queryViaMulticast` to prevent crashes from runtime exceptions and validating DNS label lengths in `isMdnsHostname` to avoid invalid query construction.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> The [consumer version of Gemini Code Assist on GitHub](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/review-repo-code) is being sunset. Starting **June 18, 2026**, new organization installations will be blocked, and all code review activity will officially cease on **July 17, 2026**.
> For more details on the timeline and next steps, please review the [Help Documentation](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/deprecations/consumer-code-review).

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

To prevent attempting mDNS resolution for hostnames with invalid label lengths (which would otherwise cause `buildQuery` to throw an `IllegalArgumentException`), we should validate that each label's UTF-8 byte length is between 1 and 63 bytes directly in `isMdnsHostname`.

```kotlin
            return labels.size >= 2 &&
                labels.last().equals("local", ignoreCase = true) &&
                labels.all { label ->
                    val bytes = label.toByteArray(Charsets.UTF_8)
                    bytes.size in 1..63
                }
```

**Actionable comments posted: 2** > [!CAUTION] > Some comments are outside the diff and can’t be posted inline due to platform limitations. > > > >
> ⚠️ Outside diff range comments (3)
> >
> app/src/main/java/org/connectbot/ui/screens/hostlist/HostListScreen.kt (1)
> > `787-788`: _🎯 Functional Correctness_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_ > > **Disconnect action unintentionally disabled while connecting.** > > Prior to this change, a bridge with an active connection attempt mapped to `ConnectionState.CONNECTED` (the old two-way logic), so the per-host "Disconnect" menu item was enabled and could cancel the attempt. Now that such bridges map to the new `ConnectionState.CONNECTING`, this `enabled` check (which only allows `CONNECTED` or `FAILED`) silently disables the ability to disconnect/cancel a host mid-connection-attempt from the list. > >
> 🐛 Proposed fix to restore cancel-during-connect capability > > ```diff > enabled = connectionState == ConnectionState.CONNECTED || > + connectionState == ConnectionState.CONNECTING || > connectionState == ConnectionState.FAILED, > ``` >
> >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the > rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. > > In `@app/src/main/java/org/connectbot/ui/screens/hostlist/HostListScreen.kt` > around lines 787 - 788, The HostListScreen disconnect menu item is now gated > only on ConnectionState.CONNECTED or ConnectionState.FAILED, which leaves > ConnectionState.CONNECTING unable to cancel an in-flight attempt. Update the > enabled condition in the per-host action logic so the Disconnect action is also > enabled while a host is CONNECTING, and keep the change localized around the > ConnectionState check that drives the menu item state. > ``` > >
> > > >
>
> app/src/test/kotlin/org/connectbot/ui/screens/console/ConsoleShortcutHandlerTest.kt (1)
> > `161-168`: _📐 Maintainability & Code Quality_ | _🔵 Trivial_ | _💤 Low value_ > > **`assertNoActions()` doesn't verify `clearSelectionCount`.** > > Currently harmless since callers only use `assertNoActions()` with `selectionActive = false`, but if it's reused later with an active selection, a regression in clear-selection behavior would go undetected. > >
> ♻️ Proposed fix > > ```diff > fun assertNoActions() { > assertEquals(0, copyCount) > assertEquals(0, pasteCount) > assertEquals(0, increaseCount) > assertEquals(0, decreaseCount) > assertEquals(0, nextPaneCount) > assertEquals(0, previousPaneCount) > + assertEquals(0, clearSelectionCount) > } > ``` >
> >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the > rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. > > In > `@app/src/test/kotlin/org/connectbot/ui/screens/console/ConsoleShortcutHandlerTest.kt` > around lines 161 - 168, The helper assertNoActions() in > ConsoleShortcutHandlerTest only checks copy/paste/navigation counters and misses > clearSelectionCount, so add an assertion for clearSelectionCount there as well. > Update the AssertNoActions helper to verify every tracked action counter, > ensuring future reuse catches regressions in selection-clearing behavior. > ``` > >
> > > >
>
> app/src/main/java/org/connectbot/service/TerminalBridge.kt (1)
> > `735-743`: _🎯 Functional Correctness_ | _🔵 Trivial_ | _⚡ Quick win_ > > **Add regression coverage for `onConnected()` timing** > The `touchHost(host)` call is in the right place, but there’s still no test that exercises successful vs. failed connection attempts and guards `lastConnect` from being bumped too early. > >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the > rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. > > In `@app/src/main/java/org/connectbot/service/TerminalBridge.kt` around lines 735 > - 743, Add regression coverage around TerminalBridge.onConnected() to verify > host-touch timing. Create tests that cover both a successful connect path and a > failed/aborted attempt path, and assert that manager.touchHost(host) only > happens from onConnected() after success, not during earlier connection setup, > so lastConnect is not updated too early. Use the TerminalBridge.onConnected() > and manager.touchHost(host) symbols to locate the behavior under test. > ``` > >
> > > >
> >
🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. Inline comments: In `@app/src/main/java/org/connectbot/service/TerminalManager.kt`: - Around line 416-423: The KDoc on touchHost is stale and refers to a “nickname” even though the method takes a Host parameter. Update the documentation for TerminalManager.touchHost to describe updating the last-connected value for the given host, and keep the wording aligned with HostRepository and TerminalBridge.onConnected so the comment matches the actual API. In `@app/src/main/java/org/connectbot/transport/MdnsProxyData.kt`: - Around line 48-65: The timeout handling in MdnsProxyData.openConnection currently only applies to Socket.connect, so mDNS lookup can consume time before the caller’s budget is enforced. Thread the caller’s connectTimeout through to MdnsResolver.resolve by adding a deadline/timeout parameter there and using it in the resolve path, then update openConnection to pass the timeout through before creating the socket connection. --- Outside diff comments: In `@app/src/main/java/org/connectbot/service/TerminalBridge.kt`: - Around line 735-743: Add regression coverage around TerminalBridge.onConnected() to verify host-touch timing. Create tests that cover both a successful connect path and a failed/aborted attempt path, and assert that manager.touchHost(host) only happens from onConnected() after success, not during earlier connection setup, so lastConnect is not updated too early. Use the TerminalBridge.onConnected() and manager.touchHost(host) symbols to locate the behavior under test. In `@app/src/main/java/org/connectbot/ui/screens/hostlist/HostListScreen.kt`: - Around line 787-788: The HostListScreen disconnect menu item is now gated only on ConnectionState.CONNECTED or ConnectionState.FAILED, which leaves ConnectionState.CONNECTING unable to cancel an in-flight attempt. Update the enabled condition in the per-host action logic so the Disconnect action is also enabled while a host is CONNECTING, and keep the change localized around the ConnectionState check that drives the menu item state. In `@app/src/test/kotlin/org/connectbot/ui/screens/console/ConsoleShortcutHandlerTest.kt`: - Around line 161-168: The helper assertNoActions() in ConsoleShortcutHandlerTest only checks copy/paste/navigation counters and misses clearSelectionCount, so add an assertion for clearSelectionCount there as well. Update the AssertNoActions helper to verify every tracked action counter, ensuring future reuse catches regressions in selection-clearing behavior. ```
🪄 Autofix (Beta) Fix all unresolved CodeRabbit comments on this PR: - [ ] Push a commit to this branch (recommended) - [ ] Create a new PR with the fixes

_📐 Maintainability & Code Quality_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**KDoc still refers to "nickname" instead of `host`.**

The expanded KDoc says "Update the last-connected value for the given nickname by passing through to [HostRepository]." but the function's only parameter is `host: Host`, not a nickname. This is stale wording left over from the doc expansion.

<details>
<summary>📝 Suggested doc fix</summary>

```diff
     /**
-     * Update the last-connected value for the given nickname by passing through
+     * Update the last-connected value for the given host by passing through
      * to [HostRepository]. Called by [TerminalBridge.onConnected] once a
      * connection has actually been established and authenticated — a failed
      * attempt (e.g. in airplane mode) must not bump the timestamp.
      * https://github.com/connectbot/connectbot/issues/386
      */
```
</details>

     * Update the last-connected value for the given host by passing through

     * connection has actually been established and authenticated — a failed
     * attempt (e.g. in airplane mode) must not bump the timestamp.
     * https://github.com/connectbot/connectbot/issues/386
     */

```

method takes a Host parameter. Update the documentation for
TerminalManager.touchHost to describe updating the last-connected value for the
given host, and keep the wording aligned with HostRepository and
TerminalBridge.onConnected so the comment matches the actual API.
```

</details>

**Propagate `connectTimeout` through mDNS resolution.** `resolver.resolve()` can spend up to ~4s in multicast retries before `socket.connect()` starts, so the caller’s timeout budget can still be exceeded. Thread a deadline into `MdnsResolver.resolve()`.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@app/src/main/java/org/connectbot/transport/MdnsProxyData.kt` around lines 48
- 65, The timeout handling in MdnsProxyData.openConnection currently only
applies to Socket.connect, so mDNS lookup can consume time before the caller’s
budget is enforced. Thread the caller’s connectTimeout through to
MdnsResolver.resolve by adding a deadline/timeout parameter there and using it
in the resolve path, then update openConnection to pass the timeout through
before creating the socket connection.
```

</details>
